title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Image Inspect History
summary: A release team must prove exactly which immutable multi-layer artifact ran even after a mutable tag moves in the registry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker image inspect history increment in image identity and layer provenance ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth.
Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Image Inspect History · Theory Docker Image Inspect History
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth. A defensible solution applies Docker Image Inspect History, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to image identity and layer provenance ledger.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-images-layers-registries-3
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: worked docker images layers registries 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: docker history reveals every original build command and secret with complete provenance. This fails the Docker Image Inspect History contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-image-inspect-history-b9vntn
  - competency: docker-image-inspect-history
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: image inspect history
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: fill the writable layer before graceful termination
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: b9vntn
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
